stemHtml: A pilot is performing a VFR flight. He/she is just about to contact ATC for clearance to cross controlled airspace when he/she hears the following radio transmission: “Mayday, Mayday, Mayday, Langen Radar, D-ILAA, engine failure, request vectors to nearest airport, position 20 nm west of Augsburg”. What should he/she do?

options.a: Contact ATC and request your clearance immediately to avoid blocking the frequency. Inform ATC of your intention to stay outside of controlled airspace.
options.b: Do not transmit on the frequency until the distress traffic is terminated or transferred to another frequency. Stay outside of controlled airspace.
options.c: Immediately respond to the Mayday message and ask for further details.
options.d: Change frequency to 121.5, and stand by for further instructions.
